name: Navigation
summary: Accessible navigation menus using the W3C APG Disclosure Navigation pattern. Provides keyboard navigation, collapsible sections, and router integration with aria-current tracking.

NavListBehavior.description: Keyboard navigation (roving tabindex) and active state tracking for navigation landmarks.

NavSectionBehavior.description: Disclosure toggle for collapsible navigation sections with aria-expanded management.

disclosureNavigation.pattern: Disclosure Navigation Menu pattern for site navigation
disclosureNavigation.adoption: NavSectionBehavior implements disclosure with aria-expanded and aria-controls

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='page' to indicate the current page within navigation
ariaCurrent.adoption: RouteLinkBehavior sets aria-current='page' on active links, NavListBehavior tracks it for roving tabindex

navigationLandmark.pattern: nav element with aria-label when multiple nav landmarks exist
navigationLandmark.adoption: nav:list attaches to nav elements, documentation recommends aria-label

rovingTabindex.pattern: Focus management using tabindex 0/-1 for keyboard-navigable widgets
rovingTabindex.adoption: NavListBehavior manages roving tabindex across navigation items

prefersReducedMotion.adoption: Demo CSS disables expand/collapse animations when reduced motion preferred

pattern.rationale: W3C APG recommends Disclosure Navigation for site navigation. ARIA menu role is for application menus, treeview is too heavy for typical sidebar nav.

ariaCurrentVsActiveClass.rationale: Screen readers announce 'current page' with aria-current. CSS class alone is invisible to assistive technology.

rovingTabindexOverActivedescendant.description: Use roving tabindex instead of aria-activedescendant
rovingTabindexOverActivedescendant.rationale: Better VoiceOver support. Same pattern used by TabGroupBehavior.

verticalDefault.description: Default orientation is vertical (unlike tabs which default to horizontal)

react.notes: NavLink only handles aria-current. Radix handles keyboard navigation too.

vue.notes: Does not set aria-current by default.

github.notes: Semantic HTML with minimal ARIA. Closest to our approach.
